task:
Task B of the scorer/team work: add a "team question packet" channel that turns the scorer's team-routed Gate-1 assumptions into a paste-able packet the founder can relay to their team, with an assume-unless-vetoed flow. Builds directly on Task A (scorer.ts already produces route:"team" for risky domain-fact assumptions; right now that degrades to founder). Do NOT build the rubber-stamp calibration tiers here (that is Task C).

Read extensions/foreman/docs/INTERNALS.md first (module map, Gate-1 flow, scorer.ts, the pure-helper + headless-test pattern, NEVER-do). Mirror the pure-module style of scorer.ts / reviewer.ts / docdrift.ts.

KEY DESIGN INSIGHT (founder-approved — implement, do not re-litigate): assume-unless-vetoed maps onto the TWO EXISTING GATES; do NOT invent new control flow or a separate park/resume. The flow is:
- At Gate 1, the scorer already routes some assumptions to "team" (domain-fact + risky). For those, build a paste-able "Questions for your team" packet and surface it alongside the plan.
- Approving Gate 1 = "assume" (work proceeds on those assumptions — already the behavior; assumptions already flow to crew via the intent contract from the earlier task).
- The founder relays the packet to their team out-of-band. If the team VETOES/CORRECTS an assumption, the founder rejects at Gate 1 or Gate 2 with the correction (existing reject→devContext path). If the team confirms (or is silent), the assumption stands. That IS assume-unless-vetoed — no blocking, no new pause state.

SCOPE — implement:
1. New pure module extensions/foreman/teampacket.ts (node-builtin-only, NO fs; caller passes data in). Mirror scorer.ts style. Exports:
   - a function buildTeamPacket(scored: ScoredAssumption[], opts?) that selects ONLY assumptions with route === "team" (reuse the ScoredAssumption type from scorer.ts — import the type only, keep pure), ranks them risk-desc (high before medium; drop low), caps the count (default 5, configurable) so the founder never relays a giant list, and renders a compact paste-able markdown block.
   - each packet item must be a SELF-CONTAINED, near-yes/no question carrying: the assumption (hypothesis framing "I'm assuming X"), its confidence, its risk band, and the scorer reasons (the evidence/why-risky). Format so a busy teammate can answer in one line. Lead with a short header like "## Questions for your team (relay these)".
   - return "" when there are no team-routed assumptions (back-compat: nothing surfaces).
   - keep the wording crew/founder-relay oriented; it is the founder who pastes it, the orchestrator does NOT contact any external system.
2. Surface the packet at Gate 1: where renderFounderPlan / the Gate-1 plan is emitted (planner.ts renderFounderPlan or index.ts Gate-1 render that already calls scoreAssumptions in Task A), also compute buildTeamPacket(scored) and append it as its own advisory section AFTER the plan/assumptions, only when non-empty. Additive only — do not change PLAN-JSON, do not block, do not alter the existing assumptions/plan rendering. Also record the packet text into the ledger gate1 event so it is recoverable.
3. Document the assume-unless-vetoed flow in INTERNALS.md (Gate-1 section): team-routed assumptions produce a relay packet; approve = assume; team veto = founder rejects with the correction at Gate 1/2; no separate pause state. (Doc-er stage will also run; the developer should still update INTERNALS so it ships even if doc-er degrades.)

TESTS (headless pure-data; add teampacket_test.sh and wire it into the .pi/foreman.json verify gate):
- buildTeamPacket: only route:"team" items included; route:"founder"/"self" excluded; risk-desc ordering; low risk dropped; cap respected (e.g. 7 team items -> 5 shown); empty -> "".
- packet item contains the assumption text, confidence, risk, and reasons.
- a grep-guard that the Gate-1 render path calls buildTeamPacket.
- Preserve ALL existing tests (scorer_test.sh, planner_test.sh back-compat, etc).

CONSTRAINTS:
- teampacket.ts stays pure/node-builtin-only and headlessly testable; import only the ScoredAssumption TYPE from scorer.ts (no runtime coupling beyond that).
- ADVISORY ONLY: no blocking, no DoD change, no PLAN-JSON schema change, no new control-flow/pause state. Reuse the existing Gate-1/Gate-2 approve/reject machinery for the veto path.
- Do NOT build rubber-stamp tiers, calibration loop, or cost-asymmetry one-click guards (Task C).
- Do not touch models.json or crew model assignments.

VERIFY: the .pi/foreman.json per-round gate runs the full headless suite incl. teampacket_test.sh; keep everything green. Pre-ship reviewer + soft doc-er run at the end as usual.

perRoundGates: verify (`grep -q "claude-opus-4-8:xhigh" extensions/foreman/crew/planner.md && bash extensions/foreman/test/planner_timeout_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/planner_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/gates_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/scorer_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/reviewer_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/docer_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/guard_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/fallback_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/ledger_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/dashboard/test/reader_test.sh`)

manifest.reason: Existing .pi/foreman.json is present and will be preserved.

summary: Added a pure team packet formatter, surfaced its output at Gate 1, logged it in the ledger, documented the assume-unless-vetoed flow, and wired headless tests into the verify gate.

summary: I'll investigate the work against the task requirements.

nits: index.ts:1722` `buildTeamQuestionPacketForPlan` re-scores assumptions independently of `renderFounderPlan`, so the scorer runs twice per Gate-1 render. Deterministic and harmless, but could share the scored result if a future refactor touches this seam.
teampacket.ts:91` if an assumption text already ends with `?`, the rendered line becomes `...foo? Is that correct?` — slightly awkward phrasing, purely cosmetic.
